content.text:
Great question! Putting sharp knives in the dishwasher seems harmless, but there are actually a few good reasons not to. Let me break them down:

## 1. The knives get dull faster
In the dishwasher, knives bump and rattle against other dishes, racks, and silverware during the wash cycle. All that jostling bangs up the sharp edge and makes it dull much quicker than gentle hand washing.

## 2. The water is way hotter and harsher
Dishwashers use very hot water and strong detergents—much stronger than the dish soap you use by hand. Over time, this can:
- Damage the blade's finish
- Cause certain steels to rust or get little spots
- Wear down the metal

## 3. It can ruin the handle
If your knife has a wooden handle, the heat and moisture can make it crack, swell, or loosen over time. Even some plastic handles don't hold up well.

## 4. It's actually dangerous
When you unload the dishwasher, you might reach in and grab a sharp blade you forgot was there—ouch! Hand washing keeps you more aware of where the sharp edge is.

## The bottom line
Washing by hand lets you be gentle and careful, keeping your knives sharp, rust-free, and safe. Just a quick wash with warm water and soap, then dry it right away!

So even though it's "just water and soap," the **heat**, **harsh detergent**, and **banging around** are what make the dishwasher tough on your good knives. 🔪
